dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires context-dependent directional bias. Mapped 'swing high/low breaks' to close > prev_high (long) and close < prev_low (short). Volume increase >50% mapped to vol_ratio_20 > 1.5 (50% above baseline). Price acceleration (>0.75 ATR) mapped directly to atr_14 > 0.75. Directional bias (3+ period trend) approximated with ADX_14 > 25 indicating established trend strength. Exit conditions use 3-period swing reversal as close crossing previous swing extremes.

Volume increase >50% mapped to vol_ratio_20 > 1.5 (50% above baseline). Price acceleration (>0.75 ATR) mapped directly to atr_14 > 0.75. Directional bias (3+ period trend) approximated with ADX_14 > 25 indicating established trend strength. Exit conditions use 3-period swing reversal as close crossing previous swing extremes.

source_rule_text: Trend-following strategies are most profitable when positive feedback dominates market behavior, characterized by imitative investor decisions that amplify initial price movements into large-scale trends rather than mean reversion.
